name: GOLFTEC Centennial
address: 6901 S Peoria St, Dove Valley, CO 80112
about: GOLFTEC is a specialized golf training studio focused on data-driven improvement through technology and expert coaching. They offer a comprehensive approach using advanced motion measurement technology, high-speed video analysis, and launch monitors to provide personalized feedback and structured lesson plans. The studio is designed for golfers seeking measurable improvement, with services ranging from swing evaluations and private lessons to club fitting and practice memberships. While the specific Centennial location page was not accessible, GOLFTEC as a brand emphasizes a professional, analytical environment focused on skill development rather than entertainment.
why_golfers_like_it: Advanced technology like OPTIMOTION and launch monitors provides data-driven swing analysis not available on the driving range
PGA-certified coaches offer personalized instruction and create actionable improvement plans
Club fitting service provides a data-driven approach to optimize equipment for an average of 21 more yards off the tee

food_and_drink.notes: No mention of on-site food service
No mention of bar service or alcohol for sale

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: While you can bring your own clubs, GOLFTEC provides equipment for lessons and fittings. Their club fitting service uses launch monitors and shaft optimization to help you find the best equipment from top brands.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: There is no mention of food or drink service at GOLFTEC, so it appears to be a training-focused environment without concessions.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: GOLFTEC's primary focus is on skill development through lessons and practice rather than full course simulation. The time for 18 holes would depend on the specific simulator setup and goals of your session.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: GOLFTEC offers junior golf programs and instruction, suggesting they cater to young golfers. However, as a training studio, the environment is more focused on skill development than entertainment.
